page: Microsoft Azure D48s_v3 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DSv3 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor operating at 2.6 GHz. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It includes 393 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks reveal a distinct contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is weak, as evidenced by bottom-quartile results in stress-ng single-core, Geekbench single-core, and single-threaded compression tests. However, the 48-vCPU configuration compensates in parallel workloads, delivering average multi-core CPU performance in stress-ng, PassMark, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory performance is a notable strength, with PassMark memory scores ranking in the top 25%, supported by average read and write memory bandwidth.

This resource profile makes the D48s_v3 highly suitable for multi-threaded applications, database operations, and static web serving, where parallel processing and memory capacity are prioritized over raw single-core speed. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the server offers high resource density and strong memory performance relative to its hardware class, making it a viable option for enterprise workloads that can leverage its 48 vCPUs without requiring specialized GPU acceleration.
description: The Microsoft Azure D48s_v3 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DSv3 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 48 dedicated vCPUs and 24 physical cores running at 2.6 GHz. It features 192.0 GB of RAM, 393 GB of local storage, and is Premium Storage capable. Benchmark data shows a clear tradeoff: single-core performance is weak, but multi-core and memory capabilities are robust, with PassMark memory scores ranking in the top 25%. This server is well-suited for multi-threaded workloads, database operations, and static web serving. It offers a balanced resource density for enterprise applications that do not require GPU acceleration.

tagline: A balanced 48-vCPU virtual machine with 192 GB RAM and local storage, delivering strong memory performance for multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
